question: Can amoebas get cancer?
context: An amoeba is a single-celled organism.
Cancer is the improper growth of a mass of cellular tissue, made of many incorrectly formed cells.
response:
<reasoning>
An amoeba is a single-celled organism.
Cancer is defined as the improper growth of a mass of cellular tissue made of many incorrectly formed cells.
A single-celled organism like an amoeba cannot form a mass of tissue.
Cancer requires multicellular organization to develop.
Amoebas cannot get cancer.
<answer> No
claims: An amoeba is a single-celled organism
Cancer is defined as the improper growth of a mass of cellular tissue made of many incorrectly formed cells
A single-celled organism like an amoeba cannot form a mass of tissue
Cancer requires multicellular organization to develop
